purpose: Compact implementation scoreboard for the CTI/DWM/XDR product lanes. This is for the 10-minute coordinator loop; it should reduce management overhead and keep workers from shipping dashboard-only slices.

antiDashboardRules: Do not ship metric cards unless there is a queue item that can be opened, reviewed, assigned, escalated, suppressed, replayed, or closed.
Do not show source counts without source health, last/next check, parser status, and a concrete operator action.
Do not show actor profiles without source links, changed fields, last refresh, next refresh, and a clear reason the profile changed.
Do not show alerts without evidence IDs, source IDs, timestamps, match reason, dedupe key, and delivery state.
Do not build webhook settings without test send, payload preview, retry history, and redacted secret handling.
Do not call a workflow autonomous unless the page exposes latest run time, changed records, next planned work, and failure/backoff state.

countsAsImprovement: A KPI value increases from a real source, real user/org action, real delivery, or real case transition and the row has a commit or run timestamp.
A blocker is removed by shipped code plus a focused smoke/probe that would fail on demo-only or default-tenant behavior.
A dirty handoff becomes an isolated commit with the row's measurable KPI wired into product-progress output.
countsAsCircularWork: Adding cards, counters, or overview copy without queue/detail/action behavior.
Moving demo/sample data around while realAlertsGenerated10m, active sources, deliveries, cases, or org usage stay unchanged.
Saying autonomous without latest run time, changed records, next planned work, and failure/backoff state.
deployCriteria: Working tree contains only the intended release diff or documented unrelated baseline.
Scoreboard scan passes and every changed lane has a focused smoke/probe.
Product-progress or handoff output names real KPI movement for orgs, sources, alerts, delivery, cases, helpdesk, audit, or deploy health.
No buyer path depends on demo-only, default-tenant, or manually curated data.
